county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Randall County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Canyon. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Canyon. Panhandle county organized 1889. PANHANDLE-PLAINS HISTORICAL MUSEUM in Canyon — the largest history museum in Texas and the premier repository for Panhandle research. West Texas A&M University archives. Palo Duro Canyon heritage. Check Bexar for pre-organization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Randall County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Canyon holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Randall County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1876 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Randall County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1876. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Randall County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1876. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Randall County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1876 to 1950 for Randall County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Randall County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Randall County from 1876. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Reagan County formed 1903 from Tom Green. County seat is Big Lake. West Texas / Permian Basin region with ranching, oil, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Big Lake. West Texas county. Santa Rita No. 1 oil well (1923) — financed University of Texas Permanent University Fund. Reagan County Historical Society. Check Tom Green for pre-1903 records. Ranching and oil.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Reagan County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1903; County Clerk in Big Lake holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1903 to 1950 for Reagan County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Real County formed 1913 from Bandera, Edwards, Kerr. County seat is Leakey. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Leakey. Hill Country county created 1913. Real County Historical Society. Frio Canyon area. Check Bandera, Edwards, Kerr for pre-1913 records. Ranching heritage. One of Texas's least-populous counties.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Real County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1913; County Clerk in Leakey holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Red River County formed 1837 from Original Texas county. County seat is Clarksville. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Clarksville. Original Texas county — one of the earliest Anglo settlement areas in Texas (1816+). Red River County Heritage Museum. Clarksville has some of the oldest records in Texas. Check for Republic-era records at TSLAC. Significant African American history. Northern Star newspaper archives.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Red River County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1837; County Clerk in Clarksville holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Red River County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1837 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Red River County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1837. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Red River County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1837. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Red River County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Red River County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Red River County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Red River County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Red River County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Red River County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Reeves County formed 1883 from Pecos. County seat is Pecos. West Texas / Permian Basin region with ranching, oil, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Pecos. Trans-Pecos county. West of the Pecos Museum (Judge Roy Bean heritage). Reeves County Historical Commission. Check Pecos County for pre-1883 records. Irrigation farming (Balmorhea area). Oil and gas.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Reeves County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1883; County Clerk in Pecos holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Reeves County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1883 to 1950 for Reeves County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease records, mineral rights transfers, and related land documents from 1920. Texas GLO maintains state mineral records. County Clerk has private mineral lease records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Reeves County from 1883. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Refugio County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Refugio). County seat is Refugio. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage. BURNED COUNTY: 1880 fire; some early records lost. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Refugio. 1880 fire destroyed some early records. Original Texas county with Spanish colonial heritage (Mission Nuestra Senora del Refugio). Refugio County Museum. Check Catholic Church sacramental records. Ranching heritage. Irish colonists (Power and Hewetson colony).
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Refugio County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in Refugio holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Refugio County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1880 to present. Some gaps due to 1880 fire; some early records lost.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1880. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1880. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Refugio County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Refugio County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Spanish Colonial / Mission Records -- Refugio County Area
source_profiles.repository: Catholic Archives of San Antonio / TSLAC / Bexar Archives at UT-Austin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Spanish colonial records including mission registers (baptisms, marriages, burials), presidio records, and land grants. Refugio County area was part of the Spanish colonial system. Records at Catholic Archives of San Antonio, TSLAC, and Bexar Archives at UT-Austin.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Colonial-era records vary in survival; many originals at Catholic Archives or in Mexican archives

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Refugio County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Roberts County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Miami. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Miami. Northeast Panhandle county organized 1889. Roberts County Museum. Ranching heritage. One of Texas's least-populous counties. Check Bexar for pre-organization records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Robertson County formed 1837 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Viesca). County seat is Franklin. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage. BURNED COUNTY: 1879 courthouse fire destroyed records. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Franklin. 1879 courthouse fire destroyed records — check TSLAC for backups. Original Texas county (Robertson's colony — Sterling C. Robertson's empresario grant). Robertson Colony records at TSLAC and GLO. Check for Republic-era records. African American community records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Robertson County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1837; County Clerk in Franklin holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Robertson County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Rockwall County formed 1873 from Kaufman. County seat is Rockwall. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Rockwall. North Texas county — the smallest Texas county by area. Rockwall County Historical Foundation. Check Kaufman for pre-1873 records. Rapid suburban growth from Dallas.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Runnels County formed 1858 from Bexar, Travis. County seat is Ballinger. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Ballinger. Central-west Texas county organized 1880. Runnels County Historical Commission. Farming and ranching. Check Bexar and Travis for pre-organization records.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Runnels County Area (various tribes)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for various tribes in the Runnels County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.
